page: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server featuring 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with a combined 640 GB of VRAM. It is powered by an Intel Xeon x86_64 CPU configuration providing 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores with a base clock speed of 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 1360.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 14.17 GB of RAM per core. No local storage is bundled with this instance type, and it includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data indicates a clear division between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance and Geekbench single-core scores, fall into the bottom 25% tier. However, multi-core and parallelized execution are highly robust, with stress-ng multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores ranking in the top 25%. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally strong, reaching 4205.12 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 193.62 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Redis SET operations also perform in the top 25%, achieving 61.66 million operations per second.

Given the massive density of 16 enterprise-grade GPUs and over a terabyte of system memory, this server represents a highly specialized, capital-intensive hardware class. The primary tradeoff is the relatively low single-threaded CPU speed, meaning workloads relying on single-core execution will experience inefficiencies. This server is designed for highly parallelized workloads that can leverage massive GPU acceleration and high memory bandwidth, such as large-scale deep learning training, complex scientific simulations, and high-throughput parallel data processing.
description: The Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g is an accelerator-optimized server designed for massive parallel workloads. It features 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 640 GB of total VRAM, paired with 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs (48 physical cores) running at 2.2 GHz and 1360.0 GB of system memory. While single-core CPU benchmarks and single-threaded compression tasks rank in the bottom 25%, the server excels in multi-core and memory-intensive operations. Multi-core stress-ng benchmarks, PassMark CPU scores, and memory read bandwidth (up to 4205.12 GB/sec) all rank in the top 25%. Additionally, Redis SET operations reach 61.66 million operations per second. Lacking local storage, this instance is highly optimized for GPU-accelerated workloads, large-scale machine learning training, and high-performance computing tasks that require substantial memory capacity and parallel processing power.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g features 16 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 1360 GB RAM. It delivers exceptional multi-core and memory bandwidth performance for parallel computing.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform a2-megagpu-16g offers 16 NVIDIA A100 GPUs, 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, and 1360 GB RAM for high-performance parallel workloads.
tagline: An accelerator-optimized platform delivering sixteen enterprise GPUs and over a terabyte of system memory for massive parallel computing workloads.
bullet_points: 16 NVIDIA Ampere A100 GPUs with 640 GB total VRAM
96 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 1360 GB of system memory
Strong multi-core performance and high memory read bandwidth
No bundled local storage or complimentary public IPv4 addresses
Best suited for deep learning training and parallel scientific simulations
